auth.confirmation.sentTo: Check your inbox — we sent a sign-in link to
auth.director.description: Whether it's your first time or you've already registered as a director, this is the place to start. As director, the stage is yours and you are in full control.
auth.actor.description: If you've already been invited, you'll receive an invitation email from the director. Just enter your email address and you're good to go!

session.description: Join the remote session to collaborate with others in real-time. Your microphone will be used for live voice interactions. You can mute your microphone at any time.

scene.noBackgroundInLibrary: No background in library to assign yet.

meta.home.title: potmagic: Live Story Theater
meta.home.description: Step into the digital spotlight. potmagic is a live collaborative storytelling platform for families, friends, and creators to perform interactive stories from anywhere.
meta.home.ogDescription: Perform live interactive stories with your community from anywhere in the world. Directors, actors, and audiences connect in real-time.
meta.home.twitterDescription: Perform live interactive stories with your community from anywhere in the world.
meta.concept.title: Concept — potmagic: Live Story Theater
meta.concept.description: Learn about the vision behind potmagic — a live story theater platform built for Directors, Actors, and Communities to create and share interactive stories in real-time.
meta.concept.ogDescription: A deliberate antidote to passive screen consumption. potmagic brings people together through live, collaborative storytelling.
meta.pricing.title: Pricing — potmagic
meta.pricing.description: Simple, honest plans for every stage. From free family shows to full professional productions — potmagic has a plan for every storyteller.
meta.pricing.ogDescription: Simple, honest plans for every stage. From free family shows to full professional productions.

terms.title: Terms & Policy
terms.intro.title: 1. Welcome to potmagic
terms.intro.body: By using potmagic, you agree to these terms. potmagic is a live story theater platform designed for creative expression and collaborative performance.
terms.accounts.title: 2. User Accounts
terms.accounts.body: You are responsible for maintaining the security of your account. Directors are responsible for the actors they invite and the content of their productions.
terms.content.title: 3. Content & Intellectual Property
terms.content.body: You retain ownership of the original stories and assets you upload. By performing on potmagic, you grant us a license to broadcast and host your content for the purpose of providing the service.
terms.conduct.title: 4. Prohibited Conduct
terms.conduct.body: You may not use potmagic for illegal purposes, harassment, or infringing on others' rights. We reserve the right to terminate accounts that violate these guidelines.
terms.liability.title: 5. Limitation of Liability
terms.liability.body: potmagic is provided 'as is'. We are not liable for any damages arising from your use of the platform or technical interruptions during live shows.
terms.changes.title: 6. Changes to Terms
terms.changes.body: We may update these terms from time to time. Your continued use of the platform after changes are posted constitutes acceptance of the new terms.
terms.contact.title: 7. Contact Us
terms.contact.body: If you have questions about these terms, please contact our stage crew at support@potmagic.live.
about.title: Our Story
about.subtitle: Where digital puppetry meets the magic of live performance.
about.mission.title: Our Mission
about.mission.body: At potmagic, we believe that distance should never be a barrier to shared creativity. Our mission is to provide families, friends, and creators with a digital stage where they can perform, connect, and tell stories together in real-time.
about.values.title: Our Values
about.values.creativity.title: Limitless Creativity
about.values.creativity.body: We provide the tools; you provide the imagination. From forests to fairy tales, the stage is yours.
about.values.connection.title: Human Connection
about.values.connection.body: It's not about the screens; it's about the voices and movements of the people behind them.
about.values.inclusion.title: Inclusion for All
about.values.inclusion.body: Theater should be accessible to everyone, regardless of physical location or technical expertise.
about.team.title: Meet the Stage Crew
about.team.body: potmagic was founded by a small team of artists and engineers passionate about the power of live performance. We're dedicated to building a space where stories come to life.
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro.body: At potmagic, we take your privacy as seriously as we take our performances. This policy explains how we collect, use, and protect your data when you use our platform.
privacy.collect.title: 1. Information We Collect
privacy.collect.body: We collect account information (email, name), performance data (stage interactions), and media assets you voluntarily upload (characters, backgrounds).
privacy.use.title: 2. How We Use Information
privacy.use.body: Your data is used to provide the live theater experience, facilitate collaboration between directors and actors, and improve our platform's performance.
privacy.sharing.title: 3. Data Sharing
privacy.sharing.body: We do not sell your personal data. We only share information with trusted service providers necessary to host our platform and process communications.
privacy.live.title: 4. Live Performances
privacy.live.body: Please be aware that potmagic is a live performance platform. Content performed on stage may be visible to other participants in your production or broadcast to an audience as intended by the director.
privacy.security.title: 5. Security
privacy.security.body: We implement industry-standard security measures to protect your data. However, no method of transmission over the internet is 100% secure.
privacy.rights.title: 6. Your Rights
privacy.rights.body: You have the right to access, correct, or delete your personal information at any time through your account settings or by contacting us.
privacy.contact.title: 7. Privacy Contact
privacy.contact.body: For privacy-related questions, please contact our stage crew at privacy@potmagic.live.
help.title: Help Center
help.subtitle: How can we help you create magic today?
help.categories.gettingStarted.title: Getting Started
help.categories.gettingStarted.body: New to the stage? Learn how to set up your first show, invite actors, and manage your theater.
help.categories.technical.title: Technical Support
help.categories.technical.body: Troubleshooting for camera, mic, and connection issues during live performances.
help.categories.assets.title: Asset Management
help.categories.assets.body: How to upload and optimize your characters, backgrounds, and props for the best performance.
help.categories.faq.title: FAQ
help.categories.faq.body: Quick answers to the most common questions about roles, payments, and features.
help.contact.title: Still need help?
help.contact.body: Our stage crew is ready to assist you. Drop us a line at support@potmagic.live.
notFound.heading: Page not found
notFound.body: The curtain has fallen on this URL. Let's get you back to the stage.

stage.empty: Currently there is nothing here. Select a story then choose a scene to enter the stage.
pricing.header.title: Simple, honest plans
pricing.header.subtitle: Whether you're gathering family around a story or running a full production, there's a plan for every stage.
pricing.plan.free.title: Curtain Up
pricing.plan.free.description: Perfect for families and small groups exploring the stage for the first time.
pricing.plan.popular.title: The Troupe
pricing.plan.popular.description: For creators and storytellers who want the full stage experience.
pricing.plan.teams.title: Grand Theater
pricing.plan.teams.description: Schools, studios, and organizations running professional productions.
pricing.earlyAccess.title: Pricing is being crafted
pricing.earlyAccess.description: The plans above are a preview. Billing and subscriptions are still under construction — everything is free during the early access period.
concept.vision.title: Live Story Telling
concept.vision.bodyIntro: In a world where screen addiction is at an all-time high and yet genuine human connection is quietly fading,
concept.vision.bodyMid: returns to the roots of active storytelling. We scroll endlessly, consume passively, and interact with feeds instead of faces — but we rarely
concept.vision.bodyConclusion: is a deliberate antidote: a space where people perform, collaborate, and truly share a moment.
concept.workshop.title: Architect the Experience
concept.workshop.subtitlePre: Every great show starts behind the curtain. Here is how a Director sets the stage for a production like
concept.workshop.step1.title: Script & Scene Mapping
concept.workshop.step1.item1.label: Narrative Flow
concept.workshop.step1.item1.text: Input story beats and define settings (e.g., The Village, The Deep Bavarian Forest, Grandma's Cottage).
concept.workshop.step1.item2.label: Atmosphere
concept.workshop.step1.item2.text: Select Background Layers (parallax scrolling art) and Music Loops (e.g., "Whimsical Forest" or "Suspenseful Growl").
concept.workshop.step2.title: Asset Curation
concept.workshop.step2.item1.label: Character Assignment
concept.workshop.step2.item1.text: Select "Digital Puppets" — 2D assets that respond to actor input.
concept.workshop.step2.item2.label: Virtual Props
concept.workshop.step2.item2.text: Add interactive elements like a "Basket of Treats" or a "Hidden Wolf" behind a tree.
concept.workshop.step3.title: Casting & Invitations
concept.workshop.step3.item1.label: Role Assignment
concept.workshop.step3.item1.text: Assign characters to specific people (e.g., Daughter as "Red," Grandpa as "The Wolf").
concept.workshop.step3.item2.label: The Call
concept.workshop.step3.item2.text: Send unique secure links containing a Digital Mask — the specific puppet that actor will control.
concept.workshop.step4.title: The Rehearsal (Sandbox)
concept.workshop.step4.item1.label: Draft Mode
concept.workshop.step4.item1.text: The stage opens for actors to test movements via mouse, touch, or motion-tracking.
concept.workshop.step4.item2.label: Live Coaching
concept.workshop.step4.item2.text: The Director uses live voice-chat to give notes and guide the performance.
concept.actor.title: Performance & Spontaneity
concept.actor.subtitle: Being an actor on potmagic is about presence, instinct, and play.
concept.actor.entry.title: Entry
concept.actor.entry.description: Click the invitation link to enter the Backstage View.
concept.actor.control.title: Control
concept.actor.control.description: Maneuver your character's silhouette or stylized puppet on screen.
concept.actor.voice.title: Voice
concept.actor.voice.description: Transmit live audio with optional Character Filters (e.g., deepening the Wolf's voice).
concept.actor.interaction.title: Interaction
concept.actor.interaction.description: Viewers can speak and interact directly with actors in real-time — no passive crowd.
concept.showtime.title: The Live Broadcast
concept.showtime.link.title: The Link
concept.showtime.link.description: A public or private Curtain Link is shared with the audience — no downloads, no sign-up required to watch.
concept.showtime.engagement.title: Live Engagement
concept.showtime.engagement.description: Viewers act as the "Village," triggering sound effects (applause, gasps) or voting on plot points (e.g., "Should Red take the shortcut?").
concept.showtime.emotion.title: Emotional Connection
concept.showtime.emotion.description: The Director orchestrates transitions live while actors provide the soul of the performance.
concept.tech.title: Technical Requirements
concept.tech.platform.title: Platform
concept.tech.performance.title: Performance
concept.tech.performance.detail: Voice and movement synchronised in real-time
concept.tech.communication.title: Communication
concept.tech.account.title: Account
concept.tech.account.detail: A working email address to create an account and receive invites
concept.tech.library.title: Asset Library
concept.tech.library.detail: Still images, sound effects, and Rive-powered animated characters
concept.cta.quote: "Distance is no longer a barrier to the magic of the theater."
docs.overview.title: Documentation
docs.overview.subtitle: Everything you need to perform, direct, and watch stories on potmagic.
docs.roles.heading: Roles
docs.role.director.title: Director
docs.role.director.description: The storyteller in charge. Creates the story, invites actors, assigns characters, curates scenes, and controls when the show goes live or ends.
docs.role.director.bullet2: Assign characters from the prop library
docs.role.actor.title: Actor
docs.role.actor.description: A performer with one assigned character per story. Joins the stage to move, rotate, mirror, and voice their character live in front of an audience.
docs.role.viewer.title: Viewer
docs.role.viewer.description: The audience. Receives a unique broadcast link and watches the live show — no account or login required.
docs.howItWorks.heading: How a Show Works
docs.lifecycle.step1.title: Director creates the story
docs.lifecycle.step1.body: A Director initializes a new story and invites participants to join as actors. Each actor is linked to the story and awaits a character assignment.
docs.lifecycle.step2.title: Characters are assigned
docs.lifecycle.step2.body: The Director picks a character from the prop library for each actor. Every actor gets one unique character per story.
docs.lifecycle.step3.title: Scenes are curated
docs.lifecycle.step3.body: The Director builds out scenes and decides which characters appear in each one — setting the stage for every act of the story.
docs.lifecycle.step4.title: Rehearsal in draft mode
docs.lifecycle.step4.bodyPre: Actors join the private stage while the story is in
docs.lifecycle.step4.bodyPost: status. The Director runs through the scenes until the story is performance-ready.
docs.lifecycle.step5.title: Show is announced
docs.lifecycle.step5.body: The Director schedules a broadcast time. An email is sent to the audience with a unique URL — no account required to watch.
docs.lifecycle.step6.title: Live broadcast begins
docs.lifecycle.step6.bodyPost: . The public can watch via their unique URL. Actors perform in real time — voice and canvas are streamed live to every viewer.
docs.lifecycle.step7.title: Show ends
docs.lifecycle.step7.bodyPre: The Director sets the story to
docs.lifecycle.step7.bodyPost: . All viewers are automatically disconnected and the broadcast URL becomes inactive.
docs.stageControls.heading: Stage Controls
docs.control.drag.label: Drag
docs.control.drag.description: Click and drag your character anywhere on the stage.
docs.control.rotate.label: Rotate
docs.control.rotate.description: Use two fingers on a touch screen to rotate and pan your character simultaneously.
docs.control.mirror.label: Mirror
docs.control.mirror.description: Double-click or double-tap to flip your character horizontally.
docs.control.voice.label: Voice
docs.control.voice.description: Your microphone is live when you join the stage. Use the mute button in the cast overlay to toggle.
docs.control.keyboard.label: Keyboard Shortcuts
docs.control.keyboard.description: Control your character's animation states via keyboard shortcuts while on stage.
docs.broadcast.heading: Public Broadcast
docs.broadcast.shareBody: When a story goes live, a public broadcast URL is generated. Share it with your audience — no login required to watch. Viewers receive the canvas stream and voice audio in real time.
docs.addScenes.title: Add Scenes
docs.addScenes.subtitle: Scenes divide your story into acts. Each scene has its own cast, backgrounds, and props — the director switches between scenes live during the performance.
docs.addScenes.whoCanAdd.heading: Who can add scenes?
docs.addScenes.whoCanAdd.bodyPost: role can add and manage scenes. Actors can view scenes they are cast in but cannot create or reorder them.
docs.addScenes.steps.heading: Adding a Scene
docs.addScenes.step1.title: Open the story detail page
docs.addScenes.step1.body: Navigate to Stories and click your story. You will see a Scenes tab listing all current scenes.
docs.addScenes.step2.title: Click "Add Scene"
docs.addScenes.step2.body: Press the Add Scene button. Enter a title for the scene — for example "Act 1" or "The Forest".
docs.addScenes.step3.title: Assign cast to the scene
docs.addScenes.step3.body: Open the scene detail page. Use the "Add to Scene" controls to choose which cast members appear in this scene. You can add one background and multiple characters per scene.
docs.addScenes.step4.title: Reorder scenes
docs.addScenes.step4.body: Drag scenes in the scene list to reorder them. The order determines the sequence during the live performance.
docs.addScenes.rules.heading: Scene Composition Rules
docs.addScenes.rule1.label: One background per scene
docs.addScenes.rule1.desc: Each scene can have at most one background prop. Adding a second background replaces the existing one.
docs.addScenes.rule2.label: Multiple characters
docs.addScenes.rule2.desc: You can add any number of character props to a scene, limited only by the number of cast members in your story.
docs.addScenes.rule3.label: Independent positioning
docs.addScenes.rule3.desc: Character positions are saved per scene. Moving a character in Scene 2 does not affect their position in Scene 1.
docs.addScenes.switching.heading: Switching Scenes Live
docs.addScenes.switching.body: During a live performance, the director uses the Scene Navigator on the stage to switch between scenes. All participants and viewers transition to the new scene simultaneously. Actors only see the stage for scenes they are cast in.
docs.createStory.title: Create a Story
docs.createStory.subtitle: Stories are the top-level container for your performance. Each story holds scenes, a cast of actors, and a live broadcast channel.
docs.createStory.whoCanCreate.heading: Who can create stories?
docs.createStory.whoCanCreate.bodyPost: role can create and manage stories. Actors can view stories they are assigned to but cannot create new ones.
docs.createStory.steps.heading: Steps
docs.createStory.step1.title: Open the Director dashboard
docs.createStory.step1.body: Navigate to the Director page from the sidebar. You will see a list of all stories you have created.
docs.createStory.step2.title: Click "New Story"
docs.createStory.step2.body: Press the New Story button in the top-right corner of the dashboard. A creation form will appear.
docs.createStory.step3.title: Enter a title
docs.createStory.step3.body: Give your story a descriptive title — for example "The Lost Crown" or "Midnight in the Forest". The title is visible to all assigned actors.
docs.createStory.step4.title: Confirm creation
docs.createStory.step4.body: Click Create. The story is created in Draft status and you are taken to the story detail page where you can add scenes and assign cast.
docs.createStory.status.heading: Story Status
docs.createStory.status.draft.desc: Default state. The stage is accessible to assigned actors for rehearsal but the public broadcast is not live.
docs.createStory.status.active.desc: The performance is live. A public broadcast URL is generated and the stage is visible to viewers.
docs.createStory.status.ended.desc: The performance has concluded. All participants are disconnected and the broadcast URL is deactivated.
docs.props.title: Props Library
docs.props.subtitle: Props are the visual and audio building blocks of your story — characters, backgrounds, sounds, and animations. Directors manage the props library and assign props to cast members.
docs.props.whoCanManage.heading: Who can manage props?
docs.props.whoCanManage.bodyPost: role can upload, rename, and delete props. Actors can view props assigned to them but cannot modify the library.
docs.props.types.heading: Prop Types
docs.props.type.character.title: Character
docs.props.type.character.description: A movable sprite that an actor controls on stage. Characters can be dragged, rotated, and mirrored.
docs.props.type.background.title: Background
docs.props.type.background.description: A full-stage image pinned to the bottom of the canvas. One background per scene. Only horizontal drag allowed.
docs.props.type.sound.title: Sound
docs.props.type.sound.description: An audio track the director can play during a scene. Audible to all participants and viewers.
docs.props.type.animation.title: Animation
docs.props.type.animation.description: A Rive animation file (.riv) placed on the canvas as an interactive animated prop.
docs.props.upload.heading: Uploading a Prop
docs.props.upload.step1.title: Go to the Director dashboard
docs.props.upload.step1.body: Open the Director page from the sidebar and switch to the Library tab.
docs.props.upload.step2.title: Click "Upload Prop"
docs.props.upload.step2.body: Choose the prop type and select a file from your device. Supported formats: PNG, WebP, SVG for images; MP3, WAV for sounds; .riv for animations.
docs.props.upload.step3.title: Name the prop
docs.props.upload.step3.body: Give the prop a descriptive name — for example "Bear Character" or "Forest Background". This name appears in the cast assignment screen.
docs.props.upload.step4.title: Assign to a cast member
docs.props.upload.step4.body: Open a story, go to the Cast tab, and assign the prop to an actor. Each actor can only hold one prop per story.
docs.props.delete.heading: Deleting Props
docs.props.delete.body: Props can be deleted from the Library tab. Deleting a prop removes it from all scenes and cast assignments permanently. Make sure to reassign actors before deleting a prop that is currently in use.
docs.sizeGuidelines.title: Size Guidelines
docs.sizeGuidelines.subtitle: Follow these guidelines when preparing prop assets to ensure characters and backgrounds look great on the stage canvas.
docs.sizeGuidelines.stageCanvas.heading: Stage Canvas
docs.sizeGuidelines.characters.heading: Character Props
docs.sizeGuidelines.char.size.label: Recommended size
docs.sizeGuidelines.char.size.desc: Characters shorter than 200 px may be hard to see; taller than 400 px may dominate the stage.
docs.sizeGuidelines.char.ratio.label: Aspect ratio
docs.sizeGuidelines.char.ratio.desc: Characters are displayed at their native size. No automatic scaling is applied.
docs.sizeGuidelines.char.format.label: File format
docs.sizeGuidelines.char.format.desc: Use transparent backgrounds (PNG or WebP with alpha) for clean character cutouts.
docs.sizeGuidelines.char.maxSize.label: Max file size
docs.sizeGuidelines.char.maxSize.desc: Keep files small for fast loading on stage join. Compress assets before uploading.
docs.sizeGuidelines.backgrounds.heading: Background Props
docs.sizeGuidelines.bg.size.label: Recommended size
docs.sizeGuidelines.bg.size.desc: Matches the stage exactly for full-bleed coverage. For panning scenes, use a wider image — e.g. 2000 × 720 px. Backgrounds are automatically pannable; actors and the director can drag them horizontally to reveal off-screen areas.
docs.sizeGuidelines.bg.position.label: Position
docs.sizeGuidelines.bg.position.desc: Backgrounds are pinned to the bottom of the canvas. Only horizontal dragging is allowed.
docs.sizeGuidelines.bg.format.label: File format
docs.sizeGuidelines.bg.format.desc: .png is suitable for photographic backgrounds without transparency requirements.
docs.sizeGuidelines.bg.maxSize.label: Max file size
docs.sizeGuidelines.bg.maxSize.desc: Backgrounds are shared with all participants — keep them compressed to reduce load times.
docs.sizeGuidelines.rive.heading: Rive Animations
docs.sizeGuidelines.rive.body: Rive files (.riv) are rendered at their exported artboard size. Export artboards at 1x scale. State machine names and artboard names must match the values set in the prop assignment panel. Keep Rive files under 1 MB where possible.
show.pin.heading: Enter your show PIN
show.pin.subtitle: You should have a 6-character PIN from the show host in your email inbox.
show.pin.error.format: PIN must contain only letters and numbers
show.pin.error.notFound: PIN incorrect, can not find story
characterBuilder.heading: Character Builder
characterBuilder.deleteImageConfirm: Are you sure you want to permanently delete this part's image? This action cannot be undone.
